metadata.title: HITRUST CSF Version 11.0 Control Catalog
metadata.remarks: HITRUST CSF (Common Security Framework) catalog containing control requirements organized by 14 control categories. This catalog supports HITRUST CSF assessments including r2, e1, and i1 assessment types.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish and maintain an information security management program (ISMP) that includes the development and maintenance of security policies, assignment of responsibilities, and ongoing monitoring and improvement.

parts.name: guidance
parts.prose: The ISMP should be comprehensive and address all aspects of information security relevant to the organization's operations, risk profile, and regulatory requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access control policies shall be established, documented, and reviewed based on business and information security requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A formal user registration and de-registration procedure shall be implemented to enable assignment and revocation of access rights.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and controlled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The allocation of secret authentication information shall be controlled through a formal management process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Asset owners shall review users access rights at regular intervals.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users shall be required to follow good security practices in the selection and use of passwords.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users shall ensure that unattended equipment has appropriate protection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users shall only be provided access to internal and external network services for which they have been specifically authorized.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Appropriate authentication methods shall be used to control access by remote users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Automatic equipment identification shall be considered as a means to authenticate connections from specific locations and equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security roles and responsibilities of employees, contractors, and third-party users shall be defined and documented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Background verification checks on all candidates for employment, contractors, and third-party users shall be carried out.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employees, contractors, and third-party users shall agree and sign the terms and conditions of their employment contract.

controls.title: Information Security Awareness, Education, and Training

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All employees and relevant third parties shall receive appropriate awareness training and regular updates.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: There shall be a formal disciplinary process for employees who have committed a security breach.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Responsibilities for performing employment termination or change of employment shall be clearly defined and assigned.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All employees, contractors, and third-party users shall return all organizational assets upon termination.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The access rights of all employees, contractors, and third-party users shall be removed upon termination.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A comprehensive risk management program shall be developed and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Risk assessments shall identify, quantify, and prioritize risks against criteria for risk acceptance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Appropriate controls shall be selected and implemented to mitigate identified risks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An information security policy shall be approved by management, published, and communicated to all employees.

controls.title: Review of the Information Security Policy

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The information security policy shall be reviewed at planned intervals to ensure its continuing suitability.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Management shall actively support security within the organization through clear direction and demonstrated commitment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security activities shall be coordinated by representatives from different parts of the organization.

controls.title: Authorization Process for Information Processing Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A management authorization process for new information processing facilities shall be defined and implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Requirements for confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements shall be identified and regularly reviewed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Appropriate contacts with relevant authorities shall be maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Appropriate contacts with special interest groups and professional associations shall be maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization's approach to managing information security shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals.

controls.title: Identification of Risks Related to External Parties

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Risks to the organization's information from business processes involving external parties shall be identified.

controls.title: Addressing Security When Dealing with Customers

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All identified security requirements shall be addressed before giving customers access to information or assets.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agreements with third parties shall cover all relevant security requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All relevant legislative, regulatory, and contractual requirements shall be explicitly defined and documented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Appropriate procedures shall be implemented to ensure compliance with intellectual property rights requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, and unauthorized access or release.

controls.title: Data Protection and Privacy of Personal Information

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data protection and privacy shall be ensured as required by relevant legislation and regulations.

controls.title: Prevention of Misuse of Information Processing Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users shall be deterred from using information processing facilities for unauthorized purposes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cryptographic controls shall be used in compliance with all relevant agreements, laws, and regulations.

controls.title: Compliance with Security Policies and Standards

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Managers shall ensure that all security procedures within their area of responsibility are carried out correctly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information systems shall be regularly checked for compliance with security implementation standards.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Audit requirements involving checks on operational systems shall be planned to minimize disruptions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Operating procedures shall be documented, maintained, and made available to all users who need them.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Changes to information processing facilities and systems shall be controlled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Detection, prevention, and recovery controls to protect against malicious code shall be implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Backup copies of information and software shall be taken and tested regularly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Networks shall be adequately managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Audit logs recording user activities, exceptions, and security events shall be produced and kept.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Procedures for monitoring use of information processing facilities shall be established.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Logging facilities and log information shall be protected against tampering and unauthorized access.

groups.title: Information Systems Acquisition, Development, and Maintenance

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Statements of business requirements for new information systems shall specify requirements for security controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data input to applications shall be validated to ensure it is correct and appropriate.

controls.title: Policy on the Use of Cryptographic Controls

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A policy on the use of cryptographic controls for protection of information shall be developed and implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Key management shall be in place to support the organization's use of cryptographic techniques.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The implementation of changes shall be controlled by the use of formal change control procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Timely information about technical vulnerabilities shall be obtained, evaluated, and appropriate measures taken.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Information security events shall be reported through appropriate management channels as quickly as possible.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All employees and third-party users shall report any observed or suspected security weaknesses.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Management responsibilities and procedures shall be established for effective incident response.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mechanisms shall enable the types, volumes, and costs of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Evidence shall be collected, retained, and presented to conform to rules for evidence in legal proceedings.

controls.title: Including Information Security in Business Continuity

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A managed process shall be developed for business continuity that addresses information security requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Events that can cause interruptions shall be identified along with their probability and impact.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Plans shall be developed and implemented to maintain or restore operations following interruption.

controls.title: Testing and Maintaining Business Continuity Plans

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Business continuity plans shall be tested and updated regularly to ensure they are up to date and effective.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The organization shall establish a privacy governance structure with defined roles and responsibilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Individuals shall be provided notice about privacy practices and choices about how their information is used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personal information shall only be used for identified purposes, retained as long as necessary, and disposed of securely.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personal information shall be protected by appropriate security safeguards against unauthorized access.
